intro: Manulea atropurpurea, also known as Purple Manulea, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Manulea atropurpurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Manulea atropurpurea is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
